evals.prompt: I want to generate 500 programmatic SEO pages for my supplement brand. Where do I start?
evals.expected_output: Asks for the proposed dimension before any generation advice. Walks the data-source-first design framework. Identifies the catalog attributes needed for per-page differentiation. Warns about the two failure modes (thinness, cannibalization). Recommends starting with a 20-page pilot before generating 500.
assertions: Asks for the proposed dimension before generation advice
Walks the data-source-first design framework
Names the two failure modes (thinness, cannibalization)
Recommends a pilot batch before generating at scale
Calls modern.sales.sku_affinity to identify natural dimension pairs
Output names a single highest-leverage next move

evals.prompt: Our agency proposed generating 'Best [ingredient] supplements in [city]' pages for 50 ingredients x 50 cities = 2,500 pages. Good idea?
evals.expected_output: Likely not. Walks search-intent-per-page-type: location intent for DTC supplements is rare and weak. Customers searching '[ingredient] supplements in [city]' usually want local stores, not DTC brands. Recommends a different dimension (ingredient x concern, or ingredient x customer segment) that matches actual DTC search behavior. Warns about cannibalization across 2,500 pages.
assertions: Pushes back on the location dimension for DTC supplements
Notes the search-intent mismatch (local-store intent vs DTC brand)
Recommends a different dimension that fits DTC behavior
Warns about cannibalization at 2,500-page scale
Walks the search-intent-per-page-type framework

evals.prompt: We generated 300 'X vs Y' comparison pages. Google indexed them but they're getting almost no traffic. Why?
evals.expected_output: Likely orphan pages. Walks the internal link graph design framework. Asks whether the pages are linked from navigational pages within 2 clicks of homepage and whether they cross-link to sibling pages. Recommends building the link graph algorithmically and adding a 'see all comparisons' index page to nav or footer.
assertions: Diagnoses orphan-page problem
Walks the internal link graph design framework
Asks about linking from navigational pages
Recommends sibling cross-linking
Recommends 'see all' index page in nav or footer

evals.prompt: Each of our 500 programmatic pages has the same template content with the keywords swapped. Is that OK?
evals.expected_output: No. Walks the data-source-first design and 'is each page actually useful' filter. Identifies the pages as template-thin and at high risk of Helpful Content downweighting. Recommends either thickening per-page content with genuine catalog data variance or pruning the page count and consolidating into fewer, denser pages.
assertions: Identifies the pages as template-thin
Names Helpful Content / quality update downweighting risk
Recommends thickening per-page content with genuine variance
Recommends consolidation as an alternative
Walks the 'is each page actually useful' filter

evals.prompt: We added FAQ to every programmatic page. Should we add FAQPage schema too?
evals.expected_output: Yes. References schema-markup for the FAQPage design. Notes that FAQPage schema + answer text is high-leverage for AI-surface visibility. References ai-seo for the AI-citation logic. Warns about Google's FAQ schema rich result restrictions (only authoritative sources qualify as of 2023+, so the schema is more useful for AI surfaces than for Google rich results).
assertions: Recommends adding FAQPage schema
References schema-markup for the design
References ai-seo for AI-citation logic
Notes Google's FAQ rich result restrictions
Frames the schema as more useful for AI surfaces than Google rich results

evals.prompt: Our 'best greens powder for athletes' and 'best greens powder for runners' pages are cannibalizing each other. Both got crushed.
evals.expected_output: Walks the cannibalization risk framework. Recommends collapsing the two into one page ('best greens powder for endurance athletes') or canonicalizing one to the other. Warns that post-launch cannibalization is hard to unwind because pages already have backlinks and signal. Recommends a one-time cleanup followed by tighter dimension design going forward.
assertions: Walks the cannibalization risk framework
Recommends collapsing into one page or canonical consolidation
Warns about post-launch cannibalization difficulty
Recommends tighter dimension design going forward
Names the high-risk band of cannibalization explicitly

evals.prompt: How often should we refresh our programmatic pages?
evals.expected_output: Depends on what's on the page. Walks the refresh cadence framework: pricing/availability monthly, SKU lists quarterly, reviews monthly, editorial annually. Recommends building the refresh pipeline into the generator. Notes that stale programmatic pages get downweighted faster than stale evergreen pages.
assertions: Walks the refresh cadence framework
Names monthly for pricing/availability
Names quarterly for SKU lists
Names monthly for review counts
Notes the faster downweighting of stale programmatic pages
